claim: OpenAI adopted Anthropic's MCP (Model Context Protocol) in March 2025 for their Agents SDK, treating it as a truly open protocol rather than proprietary, with OpenAI sitting on the MCP steering committee.
actionable: Use MCP for tool connectivity across OpenAI, Anthropic, and other platforms. It's a standardized, open protocol maintained collaboratively, not locked to any single company.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: The 'Apps SDK' inverts the traditional website-chatbot paradigm. Instead of websites with embedded chatbots, ChatGPT becomes the top layer with custom UIs and apps embedded inside it, preserving third-party branding.
actionable: When building AI integrations, think of ChatGPT as your distribution layer. Use Apps SDK to embed your app's UI directly within ChatGPT conversations, preserving your brand and user experience.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: Agent Kit is OpenAI's end-to-end solution covering Agents SDK, Agent Builder (visual workflow editor), connector registry, TrackKit, and evals. It addresses the hard problem of taking agents from prototype to production with prompt optimization and human-in-the-loop approvals.
actionable: Use Agent Builder for visual agent design, TrackKit for deployment, and evals for iterative optimization. These components work together but can be used independently.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: ChatGPT now has 800 million (or more) weekly active users and is the 5th-6th largest website in the world. It's #1-2 downloaded app on Apple App Store, making it the primary distribution channel for AI agents.
actionable: Consider ChatGPT as your default distribution platform for agent applications. The user base and seamless integration make it the path of least resistance for reaching users.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: Prompting is NOT dying. Contrary to predictions from 2 years ago, prompting has become MORE important as models improve. It's now a critical skill for production AI systems.
actionable: Invest in prompting skills. Learn systematic prompt engineering and optimization. Prompting expertise will remain valuable and differentiate builders for years to come.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: Agent Builder uses deterministic workflows (if-else blocks with Common Expression Language) alongside LLM-driven workflows. Visual nodes include human-in-the-loop approval, transform, and set state operations.
actionable: Use Agent Builder's visual nodes for complex workflows. Combine deterministic control flow (if-else, state) with LLM calls. Start simple in the playground, export to Agents SDK, or deploy via ChatKit.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: Agent Builder has templates for common patterns: customer service, document discovery, data enrichment, planning helper, structured Q&A, internal knowledge assistant. These come from OpenAI's field engineering work.
actionable: Start with templates matching your use case rather than building from scratch. These capture OpenAI's production patterns with customers.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: OpenAI's own customer support on help.openai.com is already powered by Agent Kit, demonstrating dogfooding of their own tools in production with real customers.
actionable: View Agent Kit as production-ready. OpenAI uses it internally for mission-critical customer support, validating its reliability.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: Evals for agents are still early. Initial support covers tracing multi-step workflows (20+ minute task execution), but breaking down and evaluating individual steps within traces is the next frontier.
actionable: Use evals for agent traces carefully. Expect to manually design rubrics for complex multi-step outcomes. Single-step evals work well; multi-step ones require custom evaluation logic.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: Automated prompt optimization is a core feature of Agent Kit, with evals feeding back to improve prompts over time. This is especially useful when new models launch with different behavior quirks.
actionable: Set up evals + automated prompt optimization together. As you run more tests, the system suggests prompt improvements, reducing manual prompt engineering overhead.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: ChatKit is an embeddable iframe that stays evergreen. OpenAI updates components automatically, so developers don't need to rebuild when new models or modalities (reasoning, voice) launch.
actionable: Use ChatKit for external-facing agents if you want automatic updates. Building custom chat UIs requires you to manually integrate new model features as they ship.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: First-party MCP connectors (Gmail, Notion, Stripe) require more work but provide higher quality because OpenAI can do reranking, vectorization, and state management. Third-party MCPs are more flexible but depend on MCP creator's design.
actionable: Use first-party connectors for critical business tools (email, projects) where data quality matters. Use third-party MCPs for specialized integrations where flexibility outweighs perfect optimization.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: Fine-tuning is difficult to scale at high throughput. OpenAI is exploring prompt optimization as a 'zero-gradient' alternative that achieves similar gains without managing multiple model snapshots.
actionable: Prefer prompt optimization over fine-tuning for production systems. It's faster to iterate, easier to maintain, and avoids the overhead of managing model versions.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: Service Health Dashboard tracks your API integration's real-time performance: token velocity, TPM, response codes, and custom SLOs. OpenAI recently hit 3-4 nines reliability (99.9-99.99% uptime) after significant infra investment.
actionable: Monitor your agent's API health via the Service Health Dashboard. Set up alerts for your custom SLO thresholds to catch degradation early.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: Newer LLM users (interns, younger developers) are more willing to trust models to do more in one shot, sometimes using 'full YOLO mode' which works 30-40% of the time and speeds up iteration.
actionable: Give models larger tasks than you think they can handle. Let them 'rip' without micromanaging steps. If it fails, learn from the failure; 30-40% one-shot success saves effort overall.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: Codex (OpenAI's code generation agent) is heavily used internally for PR reviews, context-switching, and kicking off tasks asynchronously. Teams kick off multiple Codex tasks during commutes, getting oriented for the day by the time they reach the office.
actionable: Use Codex for code scaffolding, reviews, and async task orientation. Kick off tasks before meetings or during commutes to accelerate context-switching.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: Agent Builder is intended for both external-facing apps (via ChatKit deployment) and internal tools. Many large enterprises have internally built agent builders; Agent Kit provides a standard platform.
actionable: If you're an enterprise, consider Agent Kit instead of building an internal agent builder from scratch. It handles template management, prompt management, and deployment.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever

claim: Plugins (2023) and GPTs failed partly because third-party developers had little control over user experience. Apps SDK learned from this by giving developers full control over branding and UI via custom components and widget builders.
actionable: When building AI products, preserve your brand and UX control. Use tools like Apps SDK that let you customize appearance and behavior, not just provide tool integrations.
source_title: DevDay 2025: Apps SDK, Agent Kit, MCP, Codex and why Prompting is More Important than Ever
